(last update: 03 Feb 2019)
The Graduation Framework is my tried and tested way of testing ads and audiences
before I'm ready to put serious money behind them.
JANUARY 2019 UPDATE
The videos below demonstrate using my PPE (Page Post Engagement) strategy to test
creatives and ads. I'd look for early traction then use this to push into a WC (Website
Conversion) campaign.
However since around mid to end of 2018 the quality of traffic from PPE, in certain countries
(US in particular) and certain audiences (Female 18-50 in particular) started to show signs of
bot or spam traffic - outbound clicks that generated very low Landing Page Views or View
Content pixel fires. This means someone clicked the ad but didn't wait for your site (and pixel)
to load.
This is a common trait of spam bots and fake profiles that people create to make it look like a
real profile for fraudulent purposes. Use this section to understand Graduation Testing then to
see how I figure out which pixel and campaign objective to use for testing.
Whether you use this model or not, the key to scaling is testing so that if (or more likely
when) scaling or optimising falters, you have ads and audiences ready to kick in.
I primarily use this methodology to test audiences, creatives and offers (which could
just be a product, category or promotion).
The Graduation Framework is a core concept to this entire course so do take the time
to learn and implement it.
It's about using small budgets to test the waters and then push them into a Website
Convert, Lead Gen, Mobile App or other key campaign objective when I'm ready.
In these videos, learn why I use (and love) this framework for testing:
- Identifying profitable audiences with low spend
- Creating an ongoing stream of pre-tested ads ready for when you need it
- What to look out for
Graduation Framework Intro
